profilePurpose: Low-sensitivity preference profile for a global mainstream-media intelligence briefing.
primaryFocus: economy / markets / central banks / financial fragility
robotics / embodied intelligence / manufacturing and supply chain
select papers and AIHOT items only when they affect broader research, industry, or culture

downrankTopics.topic: financing-only startup news without market meaning
celebrity gossip without cultural or market signal

layoutRules.itemShape: full-width row with left metadata rail and right headline, deck, and in-place expansion
layoutRules.digestMarking: low-noise editorial marks only; at most 3 core marks plus 1 data mark per item

sourceDigestRules.purpose: The expanded item should let the user understand the source without opening every link.
mustInclude: detailed Chinese reconstruction of source content
the reason this item matters beyond the headline
market, technology, culture, or capability consequence
clear separation between facts and analyst judgment

referenceStyle: paid culture and business intelligence memo
editorialVoice.tone: calm, literate, strategic, slightly sharp, evidence-aware, and judgment-led

generationGuidance.format: mobile-first premium editorial briefing ledger; Chinese main text; preserve English technical terms; layout frozen; source mix broadened to global mainstream media.
generationGuidance.selectionRule: Start from mainstream global media, then add AIHOT/arXiv/company sources only if they add a broader technology, market, robotics, or cultural signal. The daily briefing should not be dominated by large models or robotics unless the news cycle genuinely justifies it.
